zh.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral是Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton和Mrs Alan de Sylvester的兒子，於大約1145年出生。他於1170年之前與Mrs Randle de Sylvester在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1190年享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral是Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton和Mrs Alan de Sylvester的儿子，于大约1145年出生。他于1170年之前与Mrs Randle de Sylvester在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1190年享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sylvester,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from the Middle English and Latin personal name Silvester ‘dweller in the forest’ (a derivative of silva ‘wood’). The name was borne by three popes, including a contemporary of Constantine the Great, and seems to have been first used in England by clerics.

variants.name: Silvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Fernando, Juan, Miguel, Carlos, Ramon, Raul, Efren, Josefina, Mario, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.
Galician and Spanish: habitational name from any of the places called Silvestre in Pontevedra and Badajoz.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Spanish: from the personal name Silvestre (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Selwyn
meanings: English (Gloucestershire):
from the Middle English personal name Selewin(e) (Old English Selewine, perhaps from sele ‘hall’ or sǣl ‘happiness, prosperity’ + wine ‘friend’).

of Norman origin, perhaps from the Middle English and Old French personal name Selvein, Silvein (from Latin Silvanus, an adjective from silva ‘woodland’), with vocalization of /v/ to /w/, possibly through influence of the name above. In Anglo-Norman usage both Selvein and Silvester were sometimes Latinized as Salvagius ‘wild, savage’ (see Sylvester and Savage ), though whether the vernacular surnames were ever interchangeable is a question for further research.

variants.name: West
meanings: English and German: from Middle English, Middle High German west ‘west’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a settlement, or a habitational name for someone who had migrated from further west.
Americanized form of Finnish Vesterinen: from the personal name Vesteri (a short form of Sylvester ) + the surname suffix -nen.

History: This name was brought to North America independently by many bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Thomas West, 12th Baron De La Warre, was captain general of Virginia in 1610–11. The state of DE is named for him. One of the earliest permanent settlers was Francis West (1606–92), who came to Duxbury, MA, from Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, in or before 1638.

variants.name: Sylvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Pierre, Adelard, Aime, Alphonse, Carmelle, Donat, Gilles, Jean Claude, Marcel.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Sylvestre (see Sylvester ).
History: Nicolas Sylvestre dit Champagne from Pont-sur-Seine in Aube, France, married Barbe Neveu in Quebec City, QC, in 1667.

variants.name: Sule
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Aparna, Dileep, Geeta, Harsh, Pushkar, Sangeeta, Vikram.  Hungarian Mihaly, Sandor.
Indian (Maharashtra; pronounced as two syllables): Maratha name, from Marathi suḷa ‘pointed tooth’, from Sanskrit šūla ‘spike, spear’.
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Szilveszter (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Silvester
meanings: English and German: variant of   Sylvester  .

variants.name: Silvestro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Angelo, Sal, Caesar, Agostino, Antonio, Camillo, Carmelo, Dino, Ennio, Gaetano, Giovanni.
Italian: from the personal name Silvestro, Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Sylvest
meanings: Danish: from a personal name, a shortened form of Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Wirral
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Worrall  .

variants.name: Worrall
meanings: English: variant of   Worrell  .

en.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral was born about 1145 as the son of Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton and Mrs Alan de Sylvester. He married Mrs Randle de Sylvester before 1170, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1190, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral est né environ 1145, fils de Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton et Mrs Alan de Sylvester. Il a épousé Mrs Randle de Sylvester avant 1170, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1190, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral wurde um 1145, als Sohn von Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton und Mrs Alan de Sylvester geboren. Er hat vor 1170 in England Mrs Randle de Sylvester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1190 im Alter von 46 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral è nato il circa 1145 come figlio di Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton e Mrs Alan de Sylvester. Lui sposato Mrs Randle de Sylvester prima del 1170, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1190, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirralは約1145年にAlan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of StourtonとMrs Alan de Sylvesterの息子として生まれた。1170年以前にイギリスでMrs Randle de Sylvesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1190年に46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral(은)는 대략 1145년 Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton(와)과 Mrs Alan de Sylvester의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1170년 이전 영국에서 Mrs Randle de Sylvester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1190년 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral nasceu aproximadamente 1145 filho de Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton e Mrs Alan de Sylvester. Ele casou-se com Mrs Randle de Sylvester antes 1170, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1190, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral родился около 1145 г. Его родителей звали Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton и Mrs Alan de Sylvester. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Randle de Sylvester перед 1170 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1190 г. в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral nació aproximadamente 1145 como hijo de Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton y Mrs Alan de Sylvester. Se casó con Mrs Randle de Sylvester antes de 1170, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1190, a la edad de 46 años.
